title: C# Data Types
content:
### Data Types in C#

C# is a strongly typed language with two main categories of data types: value types and reference types.

### Value Types

Stored directly on the stack. Each variable has its own copy of the data.

| Type | Size | Range | Example |
|------|------|-------|---------|
| `byte` | 8-bit | 0 to 255 | `byte b = 100;` |
| `short` | 16-bit | -32,768 to 32,767 | `short s = 1000;` |
| `int` | 32-bit | -2.1B to 2.1B | `int i = 100000;` |
| `long` | 64-bit | -9E18 to 9E18 | `long l = 100L;` |
| `float` | 32-bit | ~7 digits precision | `float f = 3.14f;` |
| `double` | 64-bit | ~15-16 digits precision | `double d = 3.14;` |
| `decimal` | 128-bit | ~28-29 digits precision | `decimal m = 3.14m;` |
| `bool` | 8-bit | true or false | `bool b = true;` |
| `char` | 16-bit | Single Unicode character | `char c = 'A';` |

### Reference Types

Stored on the heap. Variables hold a reference (memory address) to the data.

| Type | Description | Example |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `string` | Sequence of characters | `string s = "Hello";` |
| `object` | Base type of all types | `object o = 42;` |
| `dynamic` | Bypasses compile-time checking | `dynamic d = "test";` |
| `class` | User-defined reference type | `class Car { }` |
| `array` | Collection of same-type elements | `int[] arr = {1, 2, 3};` |

### Nullable Types

Value types can be made nullable with `?`:

```csharp
int? nullableInt = null;
bool? isComplete = null;
double? price = 10.99;
```

> **Key Point:** Use `int` for general integers, `double` for decimals, and `decimal` for financial calculations that require high precision. `string` is a reference type but behaves like a value type in many ways.

### Default Values

| Type | Default Value |
|------|---------------|
| Numeric types | 0 |
| bool | false |
| char | '\0' |
| string | null |
| Reference types | null |

quiz.question: Which data type is best for financial calculations requiring high precision?
Which of the following is a value type in C#?
